title: Supreme Court’s intervention offers fresh hope to excluded electors for West Bengal Assembly polls
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee urged all deleted voters to apply to the tribunals and assured them that they will get their rights reinstated; she said a supplementary list for the first phase of elections will be published on 21st April and the second phase on April 27, as per the Supreme Court order

title: Parliament special sitting LIVE: Amit Shah says all elections till 2029 to be held based on existing number of seats
content: The law of the Delimitation Commission is exactly according to the old law. No changes have been made in it. The ongoing elections will not be affected by this, says Union Home Minister Amit Shah

title: Finally, Rohit Vemula Bill to safeguard SC/ST students gets Cabinet nod
content: The Bill lists out about 30 ways in which atrocities may be committed against an SC/ST student in a higher education institution

title: Call all-party meeting to evolve consensus on women’s reservation: K.C. Venugopal
content: The government’s intention was to push through delimitation in the garb of the Women’s Reservation Bill, the Congress general secretary says in Lok Sabha; he adds that PM Narendra Modi did not mention the word “delimitation” even once in his speech during the debate on the Bill, when that was the main issue being raised by the Opposition

title: With BDS student’s death in Kannur, caste bias back in focus
content: The death of a student of Kannur Dental College has raised disturbing questions about caste discrimination on Kerala’s college campuses. The college has dismissed a faculty member and suspended another, pending inquiry. The incident led to an outpouring of related complaints from other students. The bereaved family, the student’s college-mates, faculty, and activists express their views

title: Uttarakhand government orders probe into alleged movement of children to madrasas in State
content: Parag Madhukar Dhakate, Special Secretary in the Minority Welfare Department, has directed district magistrates of Dehradun, Haridwar, Udham Singh Nagar and Nainital to undertake extensive verification drives across all madrasas in their jurisdictions.

title: It’s time women are seen as lawmakers, say women MPs
content: While endorsing their party line, women legislators also cite the positive role of male founders and leaders of their parties in enabling the participation of women in jobs, education, and politics

title: J&K, Assam are victims of the delimitation process: PDP
content: We were expecting the census to include Schedule Caste and Scheduled Tribes; the government should have waited for it

title: Akhilesh Yadav urges government to implement women’s quota after caste census
content: The Samajwadi Party chief says the women’s reservation law should be implemented after a caste census so that representation for women from backward communities can be ensured in a fair manner

title: On the Sabarimala temple entry case
content: As a Supreme Court Bench examines the broader implications of its 2018 Sabarimala ruling, the case prompts a look into the evolution of the essential religious practice doctrine, role of the State and the judiciary in religious reform, and the arguments over whether Ayyappa followers qualify as a religious denomination

title: Silence in legislations on maintaining proportionality makes TDP reach out to Home Minister, Law Minister
content: TDP wanted necessary amendments to clear the ambiguity, but with Home Minister Shah’s assurance in Lok Sabha on the issue, the party unlikely to press the issue further

title: Safeguard for States with stable populations is being removed with immediate effect
content: Bills seek to start the reallocation of seats and delimitation immediately, without 2026 sunset proviso

title: Empowerment of BC leaders to strengthen party: TDP leader
content: TDP senior leader Muddada Madhu hailed the party’s high command for providing several top posts to BC leaders of North Andhra region during the reshuffle of the party posts

title: In 2025 verdict, Supreme Court had warned of delimitation ‘destabilising uniform electoral framework’
content: The Supreme Court has consistently held that though judicial review of delimitation was limited, the court could intervene if the exercise was capricious and unreasonable

title: Vijay unveils TVK manifesto, criticises Dravidian majors’ poll promises
content: He asserts that his party has only promised what was possible, practical, and implementable if voted to power

title: Delimitation Bill will not affect Tamil Nadu: AIADMK leader Edappadi Palaniswami
content: When Union Minister Amit Shah visited Tamil Nadu, he assured that the State will not be affected, stated Mr. Palaniswami. “The NDA will implement delimitation better than what Mr. Stalin expects. The Centre is clear that no State will be affected by the delimitation,” he added.

title: Revanth’s hybrid model for delimitation enters national discourse
content: The suggestion entered the national discourse, particularly amid concerns among southern States over potential loss of parliamentary representation

title: Supreme Court asks Uttar Pradesh to consider plea flagging risk of liquor cartons in educational campuses
content: A Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant directed the State government to accept a representation challenging the administrative decision made in February last year under the State excise policy allowing the manufacture, packaging and sale of liquor in small cartons

title: Priyanka Gandhi warns move towards delimitation is an ‘open attack on democracy’
content: The women’s quota could be implemented now, the Congress leader says, accusing the Union government of using the Constitution amendment to reshape political representation

title: Supreme Court says time is now too short to look into ‘mass transfer’ of high-ranking West Bengal officials ahead of election
content: The petition challenges the ECI’s decision to transfer ‘wholesale’ 63 IPS officers and 16 senior bureaucrats, including the Director General of Police and the Chief Secretary, in the ‘garb of poll-preparedness’

title: Watch: Delimitation row erupts in Parliament: What happened? | Above the Fold | 16.04.2026
content: In our new and third episode of Above the Fold, we look at the stormy session in the Lok Sabha as the Centre and Opposition clash over delimitation. Joshua Landis weighs in on the escalating West Asia conflict, while Donald Trump sparks controversy with a viral post. Also, a key Supreme Court of India observation on voting rights and R Vaishali clinches a major chess title.

title: SC directive sparks renewed hopes of crackdown on commercial activities in residential layouts
content: While the corporations are awaiting official notice and see the development as an opportunity to increase revenue generation, citizens question how this will be different, as such violations are rampant in the city and the civic body has failed to enforce regulations

title: Over 2,800 students from A.P. clear NPTEL exam on quantum computing
content: Qualified students can download their certificates through APSCHE portal https://apsche.ap.gov.in after entering their hall ticket numbers

title: Support Delimitation Bill if Odisha’s interests are protected, Naveen urges MPs from State
content: Former Odisha Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik says States such as Odisha, which have made significant strides in population control and human development, are being penalised

title: Heat stress poses serious risk to vulnerable populations in Kerala
content: The elderly and those with chronic kidney disease are at heightened risk of heat-related illnesses. Heat stress and dehydration can lead to severe complications for those on multiple medications. Medical experts warn that the elderly may struggle to regulate their body temperature effectively.

title: Government pushes for free MMTS travel to boost use of public transport
content: The high-level interaction primarily focused on resolving pending issues related to land acquisition, shifting of utilities and other bottlenecks hampering the construction of several proposed road overbridges and road underbridges (ROB/RUBs) within and outside the capital region

title: Housing for roadside dwellers and traffic solutions are top priorities for Harbour constituency, says Minister Sekarbabu
content: Minister P.K. Sekarbabu says 3,000 multi-storey housing units are planned to be built and a multi-level car parking facility being constructed at Broadway will reduce traffic congestion in areas like Sowcarpet. He also seeks to increase the number of educational institutions, learning centres, and libraries and provide uninterrupted electricity, safe drinking water, and proper sewer systems;

title: West Asia conflict eclipses election debates among Muslims here
content: Iran draws near-universal sympathy in Ramanathapuram, though the Muslim population is largely not Shia

title: Road accident victims will get treatment worth ₹1.5 lakh free in hospitals, says Health Minister
content: PM RAHAT scheme will help save lives by ensuring timely treatment, it will be available at 656 government and private hospitals across A.P., announces Satya Kumar Yadav; calls for collective efforts by Transport, Health, and  Police departments to bring down the number of accidents

title: Assembly Elections 2026 highlights: TVK releases its election manifesto
content: PM Modi urged people to come together to build a state where "the poor have shelter, food security is ensured, women feel safe, and youth can find jobs without leaving the state."

title: ACB uncovers widespread irregularities at 13 MeeSeva centres across Telangana
content: The raids which began at 4 p.m., continued into early hours as officials probed overcharging, agent nexus and suspected malpractices

title: Spoelstra: No need to penalize Ball any further
content: Heat head coach Erik Spoelstra said Thursday that LaMelo Ball didn't need to be "penalized more moving forward" in the wake of the Hornets star's fine for tripping Miami's Bam Adebayo.

title: Luka, Cunningham win appeal, eligible for awards
content: Luka Doncic and Cade Cunningham are eligible for season honors after their challenge of the 65-game rule was approved, while Anthony Edwards' challenge was declined.

title: Report: Penix, Njoku among targets in fraud case
content: A former Alabama defensive end is accused of using wigs and fake IDs to impersonate NFL players to secure nearly $20 million in fraudulent loans, with a report identifying Michael Penix Jr., David Njoku and Xavier McKinney as the targeted players.

title: Jones seeks change of plea in NBA gambling case
content: Damon Jones has requested a change-of-plea hearing for charges he provided sports bettors with nonpublic information about injuries to stars LeBron James and Anthony Davis, ABC News reported Thursday, citing a court filing.

title: Chiefs GM expecting a lot of trades in first round
content: Chiefs general manager Brett Veach said Thursday that there will "probably be a lot of trades" in the first round of this year's NFL draft.

title: Goal king Ovechkin 'pretty sure' he'll keep playing
content: Capitals star Alex Ovechkin, the NHL's all-time leading goal scorer, said he's "pretty sure" that Tuesday's season finale won't be his last game as he ponders retirement.

title: Tiger fighting subpoena for prescription records
content: Tiger Woods' attorney is fighting prosecutors' attempts to subpoena the 15-time major champion's prescription drug records from a pharmacy, according to records.

title: Homa: Antics like Garcia's are 'bad look' for golf
content: Asked about the antics like Sergio Garcia's outburst at the Masters, Max Homa said it casts a bad look on professional golfers but also acknowledged that it's a sport that can stir up those emotions.

title: 👀 Inside the making of Mariners' Ichiro statue
content: You know about the reveal mishap. Here's what went into creating the sculpture that will forever honor a Seattle icon.

content: A week before the draft, Ben Solak hits the top priorities for every single team -- and the prospects who can meet those needs.

content: With eight title bouts in two nights, WrestleMania 42 is stacked. Get caught up before this weekend's PLE.

content: A look back 10 years later at the selection of Jones, one of the most important picks in franchise history.

content: After a devastating blowout loss in Oklahoma City on April 2, the Lakers lost two of their three stars. Now they face Kevin Durant and the Houston Rockets -- as a home underdog.

title: What makes Arizona QB Noah Fifita tick? Loyalty, generosity
content: Fifita, who runs monthly community events, is one of just five senior QBs nationally who has not transferred.

title: Semifinal predictions: Don't bet against Arsenal despite misfiring attack
content: The UEFA Champions League quarterfinals largely lived up to expectations, with three of four games going down to the wire. Now that the dust has settled, let's break down the semifinals and predict who advances.

content: Jordan Chiles enters her final NCAA championships as the most recognizable face in gymnastics -- and hopes to lead UCLA to its first title since 2018.

content: In July, a Jet Ski accident caused the amputation of Katie Guillory's foot. But it only strengthened her drive.

content: WNBA free agency is here. Kelsey Plum is returning to Los Angeles. Kayla McBride is returning to Minnesota.

content: Newcastle United could sack manager Eddie Howe and turn to Jose Mourinho. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Alycia Baumgardner's journey to become the face of women's boxing
content: An early setback and a positive test -- since cleared -- didn't define Baumgardner. Now, she's on the doorstep of leading the sport.

title: The South after delimitation: Shah assures seats will increase; here's what the math shows
content: Parliament buzzed with debate over India's representational future as the delimitation exercise and Women's Reservation Bill took center stage. Prime Minister Modi and home minister Shah assured southern states no injustice would occur, projecting increased seats and influence.  Maths indicate a marginal shift in overall share despite seat increases for Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala.

title: Israel-Lebanon agree to 10-day truce: What it means for Iran-US peace talks
content: US President Donald Trump has brokered a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, inviting both leaders to the White House for crucial talks. This move aims to de-escalate regional tensions after months of heightened conflict. While battlefield clashes with Iran have eased, economic and strategic pressures persist, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz, keeping the region on edge.

title: The Art of the Deal meets the Sermon on the Mount
content: The Trump White House has opened a new front against the Vatican, with Pope Leo responding to criticism of his theological stances and implicit critiques of U.S. foreign policy. The exchange escalated amid viral imagery of President Trump as a savior figure, prompting the Pope to emphasize peace and responsible governance, while U.S. Vice President J.D.

title: IPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav memes break internet after another flop show
content: Suryakumar Yadav faced intense backlash after a first-ball duck against Punjab Kings, with fans questioning his form and approach. His dismissal left Mumbai Indians struggling early. Strong performances from Quinton de Kock and Naman Dhir highlighted missed responsibility, increasing pressure on Surya to deliver in crucial matches.

title: 'False narrative': What Amit Shah said on oppn's claims on women's quota, delimitation
content: Speaking during the debate on the women’s reservation-related amendment bills and the proposed delimitation framework, Shah said the total strength of the Lok Sabha would rise to 816 seats, though the legislation mentions a ceiling of 850 because that figure is rounded. He explained that 816 reflects a precise 50 per cent increase over the current 543 elected seats.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection day 29: Ranveer Singh film marches past Rs 1,100 cr
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has officially breached the Rs. 1,100 crore net milestone in India on Day 29. From its massive Rs. 90 crore opening day to becoming the first Hindi film to enter the Rs. 1,000 crore club, Aditya Dhar’s spy thriller starring Ranveer Singh is rewriting history. Read on to learn more about the latest live tracking updates, day-wise collection breakdown for Week 1, 2, and 3 and a recap of the worldwide gross hitting Rs. 1,733 crore despite the IPL 2026 craze.

title: Kangana says ‘Dhurandhar’ revived Bollywood, praises R Madhavan
content: Kangana Ranaut has credited the Dhurandhar franchise for breathing new life into the Indian film industry, calling it a much-needed reset at a time when Bollywood was struggling to stay relevant. Speaking about the success of the Dhurandhar: The Revenge, the actor said the film has reignited audience interest and bridged the gap between cinema and viewers.

title: 'Women can tell when they're being misled': Priyanka targets PM over quota bill
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra criticized the government's women's reservation and delimitation bills in Lok Sabha, calling them an "open attack" on democracy. She questioned the delay in implementation and alleged the government is diverting from the core issue of women's reservation, while reaffirming Congress's support.

title: How change in H-1B selection system has been a boon to employees already in the US
content: US businesses are witnessing significantly higher H-1B visa selection rates, with some reporting over 50% success. This surge follows the Department of Homeland Security's shift from a random lottery to a wage-weighted system, prioritizing higher salary offers. The new rule, effective February 27, 2026, aims to reshape the selection process for skilled foreign workers.

title: de Kock surpasses legendary MS Dhoni in first match for MI in 5 years
content: Mumbai Indians batter Quinton de Kock opened for Mumbai Indians after five years, replacing injured Rohit Sharma. He scored a quick fifty off 28 balls and surpassed MS Dhoni with his 25th IPL fifty as a wicketkeeper. His knock steadied MI amid absences, including Mitchell Santner, highlighting his experience and importance.

title: Tamil Nadu elections: Will TVK’s solo gamble split votes or fulfil Vijay’s CM dream?
content: Actor Vijay's new political party, TVK, is making a bold solo bid in the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections, challenging the state's long-standing alliance-driven politics. Historically, success hinges on coalitions, not standalone appeal. TVK's gamble aims for direct leadership, but faces the challenge of converting visibility into seats without established networks, potentially acting as a vote-splitter.

title: 'Choose poorly ... face blockade & bombs': Hegseth warns Iran to accept US peace deal
content: The US has issued a stark warning to Iran, presenting a choice between a negotiated deal and continued blockade with potential military strikes. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth stated that failure to comply could lead to "bombs dropping on infrastructure.

title: Lightning-Canadiens playoff opener delayed over arena and broadcast conflicts
content: Playoff opener between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa bay lightening has been postponed because of issues with arena availability and the broadcast schedule. The shift moves Game 1 to Sunday to give both teams more time to prepare. Having rested key players and having all logistics done, the next question is how the re-worked schedule would affect the early momentum in this much-hyped NHL game.

title: Dianna Russini’s divorce remarks resurface amid Mike Vrabel affair rumors
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini, the popular NFL insider, have millions fuming ever since their pictures from a luxurious hotel in Arizona went viral, a few days ago. While both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied affair rumors, the allegations refuse to die down. Amid all the mess, Dianna Russini has spoken about some issues in her marriage.

title: Aryna Sabalenka vs Coco Gauff: Who is the richer tennis icon
content: Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff are two of the biggest stars in women’s tennis today. Both icons have won Grand Slam titles. They have ruled on the top of the WTA rankings. There is no doubt that they are among the highest earners in the sport. While Sabalenka dominates with power and consistency, Gauff shines with speed and confidence.

title: 'Sooryavanshi future face of Indian cricket': Modi's bold prediction
content: Former IPL chairman Lalit Modi praised Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as a future star, recalling his disbelief at the teenager’s talent. Speaking to Michael Vaughan, Modi said the youngster’s confidence stood out. While Vaughan flagged challenges of fame, Modi noted the IPL’s role in creating opportunities and inspiring many young cricketers across India.

title: The umarells: Retired elderly men in Italy quietly supervise construction sites
content: Italy's cities feature "umarells," retired men who observe construction sites with hands clasped behind their backs. These "little men," originating from Bolognese dialect, offer unsolicited advice and engage with workers, blending humor and a desire for connection. This cultural phenomenon has even led to some municipalities involving them as volunteers in monitoring public works.

title: IPL: Arshdeep scripts history, becomes first Punjab Kings bowler to...
content: Arshdeep Singh delivered a match-turning spell for Punjab Kings, taking two wickets in two balls against Mumbai Indians. He dismissed Ryan Rickelton and Suryakumar Yadav to reach the milestone of 100 IPL wickets. Arshdeep joined an elite group of left-arm pacers like Trent Boult and Zaheer Khan, giving Punjab early control and shifting momentum decisively.

title: India’s big nuclear leap: How Stage 2 criticality will power the road to energy security
content: India's nuclear ambitions have reached a pivotal stage with the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor achieving criticality, marking a significant step in its three-stage nuclear program. This advancement positions India as a leader in advanced nuclear technology, bolstering its drive for energy self-reliance and security amidst a rapidly expanding power sector.

title: 'Chanakya would've been shocked': Priyanka's quip draws Shah's reaction - watch
content: Backing the idea of reserving seats for women legislators, Priyanka said her party was not opposing women’s representation itself, but questioning what she described as the political intent behind the timing and framing of the bill. She argued that the ruling NDA was using the measure as an electoral strategy rather than as a purely reform-driven step.

title: Jharkhand JAC Class 8 result 2026 released: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: Jharkhand Academic Council has announced Class 8 results for 2026. Students can now access their scorecards online by entering their roll code and number on the official websites. The scorecard contains vital details like subject-wise marks and qualifying status. It is advised to verify all information and save a copy for future reference.

title: Behind Vaishali's Candidates glory is a 19-year-old boy named Pranesh
content: 2025 saw third edition of Chennai Grand Masters. R Vaishali, the nation's toast at the moment, was invited to compete in the Challengers section, which includes relatively lower-rated players than the Masters field. Vaishali had a nightmarish tournament, finishing rock bottom.  The winner of the Challengers section was Munirethinam Pranesh, a baby-faced assassin on the board.

title: Vancouver Canucks depend on Elias Pettersson amid NHL offseason uncertainty
content: Even though there are more and more rumors about trades and questions about performance, Elias Pettersson is still the most important person in charge of the Vancouver Canucks' offseason, even though he has had great scoring seasons in the past, he has not been as productive lately. This has caused a lot of talk in the National Hockey League. The Canucks now have to make a big choice about their star center, who is worth $11.6 million, as well as their players, coaches, and long-term plans.

title: 'Bhoot Bangla' preview: What to expect from Akshay Kumar film
content: Akshay Kumar marks a strong comeback with the horror‑comedy Bhoot Bangla, rolling out paid previews on April 16 and a full release on April 17. Set in a haunted mansion, the film mixes laughs, scares and family chaos, positioned as a light‑hearted masala entertainer rather than pure horror, with strong early advance‑booking figures signalling a hopeful box‑office start after a quiet patch since December 2025.

title: ‘32 Ferraris’: Michael Vaughan left speechless by Lalit Modi’s lavish collection
content: Former England captain Michael Vaughan was astonished as Lalit Modi revealed his extensive Ferrari collection during an interview. Modi gifted his son a Ferrari every year from age 21, amassing 11 cars for him, while Modi himself owns 21. The conversation also touched upon emerging cricket talent like Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

title: Why is Rohit Sharma not playing in MI vs PBKS match?
content: Uncertainty surrounded Rohit Sharma before MI’s clash with Punjab Kings after his hamstring injury. He skipped training, and scans raised concerns. At the toss, Hardik Pandya confirmed Rohit would miss multiple games. Mitchell Santner was also unavailable, forcing changes, with Quinton de Kock returning and a debut for Mayank Rawat.

title: 'Turned into hub of terror, mafia rule': Yogi Adityanath targets Mamata-led TMC govt
content: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath drew parallels between Bengal's current situation and UP's past, highlighting law and order issues, mafia rule, and corruption. He accused the TMC of creating an identity crisis and appealed for a BJP government to end terror and corruption in West Bengal.

title: 'Not ready to take that step': Lebanon's president declines talks with Netanyahu
content: Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun has reportedly refused to speak with Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu. This comes after US President Donald Trump suggested the leaders would hold talks. Lebanese officials informed the US and Israel that negotiations will not proceed until a ceasefire is achieved. Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues despite recent discussions.

title: 'Cried day before pre-boards': CBSE Class 10 topper Gunnika Khurana turned anxiety into success
content: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) declared the Class 10th result on April 15, 2026, a day that once again brought the familiar rush of anxiety, anticipation, and constantly refreshing screens. Among the toppers was Gunnika Khurana, who secured 99.6% and spoke about a journey shaped more by consistency and calm than pressure. From early preparation in grade nine to moments of pre-board anxiety, her story reflects discipline, self-belief, and quiet resilience behind a near-perfect score.

title: 'Politicisation by Pakistan': Lalit Modi blames PCB for India-Pak cricket freeze
content: Former IPL chairman Lalit Modi expressed pessimism about India and Pakistan resuming bilateral cricket soon, citing significant political interference within the Pakistan Cricket Board as the primary obstacle. He believes external pressures have politicized decisions, hindering any possibility of a series, even at neutral venues, and fears the situation will worsen.

title: Did Pete Hegseth quote Pulp Fiction verse at prayer meet in Pentagon?
content: At a Pentagon worship service, US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth recited what he called “CSAR 25:17,” presenting it as a military prayer tied to combat search-and-rescue missions. He suggested it was meant to reflect Ezekiel 25:17, which is where the confusion begins.

title: Pune’s 169-year-old bakery serves iconic bread once loved by Indira Gandhi, Dilip Kumar
content: A Pune institution since 1857, offers a taste of tradition with its iconic, fragrant roat. This family-run bakery, now in its fifth and sixth generations, continues to craft the sweet, rich roti using simple ingredients, maintaining a legacy of honest food and cherished memories for its loyal customers.

title: Is Suvendu Adhikari BJP's Chief Minister Face In Bengal? What He Told NDTV
content: "I am not interested... I am not interested in these questions. Modi ji, Central leadership... he will make a Bengali, no problem," Suvendu Adhikari said in an exclusive interview in "Walk the Talk".

title: Parliament Special Session LIVE: Lok Sabha Proceedings Extended Till Midnight Amid Debate Over Women's Reservation Bill
content: Parliament's 3-day special session Live Updates, Day 1: The government is expected to introduce three amendment Bills aimed at operationalising the legislation, which was passed in 2023 to provide 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State

title: Jobs, Clean Governance: Suvendu Adhikari On What BJP Will Deliver In Bengal
content: Speaking on NDTV's Walk the Talk, recorded in the Bhowanipore constituency, BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari positioned himself as the face of the party's campaign against the Trinamool Congress and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee.

title: Asim Munir In Iran To Discuss Next Round Of US Talks
content: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt had told reporters that further talks "would very likely" be in the Pakistani capital. "Those discussions are being had," Leavitt said, and "we feel good about the prospects of a deal."

title: "I Was Blaring Siren, Why Didn't You Move?" MLA's Son After Ramming 5 With Thar
content: The accused's father, BJP MLA Pritam Lodhi, acknowledged the gravity of the incident, stating that the law must take its course.

title: 'Pressured Women To Join Them In Hotels': Big Claim In TCS 'Conversion' Case
content: The claims describe a conversion syndicate and a sexual harassment network operating inside TCS, one of India's largest IT firms.

title: Amit Shah Explains 850-Seat Lok Sabha Number After Opposition Criticism
content: Amit Shah said Tamil Nadu will get 20 more seats, Kerala 10, Telangana 9 and Andhra Pradesh 13 seats. Maharashtra, which after Uttar Pradesh, has the second highest number of MPs in the Lok Sabha, will gain 24 more seats.

title: "Not My Problem": Iran Mocks Trump's Hormuz Attempts In Minions-Style Video
content: In the clip, a minion version of Trump is seen floating on a wooden plank beside an American flag. "I'm going to count to three. When I open my eyes, the strait must be open," the character says.

title: Women's Reservation Bill To Remain A Post-Dated Cheque Forever: K Kavitha
content: With the Women's Reservation Bill, she alleged, the BJP intends to garner women's votes in poll-bound West Bengal.

title: Competition Panel Rejects Complaint Against Adani Group, Azure Power In Solar Tender Case
content: Rejecting the complaint, the Competition Commission of India (CCI), in its order, observed that the country's power generation market comprises many significant public and private players, and that Adani Group, prima facie, does not appear to be a dominant player.

title: Bhopal Woman, 64, Duped Of Rs 26 Lakh In Digital Arrest Fraud
content: The accused contacted the elderly woman through a WhatsApp call, impersonating officials from the Internal Security Department and the Counter-Terrorism Department.

title: Probe Agency Raids 32nd Avenue Group In Rs 500 Crore Scam, Seizes Cash, Gold
content: New Delhi, April 16 (IANS In fresh action in a Rs 500 crore ponzi scheme fraud, the ED searched seven premises across Delhi-NCR, Goa, Jaipur and Mumbai linked to 32nd Avenue Group and seized Rs 1.05 crore cash and jewellery items worth Rs 1.5 crore,

title: Top Court Allows Last-Minute Inclusion Of 'Cleared' Voters In Bengal Rolls
content: "If the appeals are adjudicated by April 21 for the first phase and April 27 for the second phase then a supplementary revised electoral roll must be issued," the Supreme Court told the Election Commission.

title: Woman Backs Out Of Divorce Settlement, Irked Top Court Dissolves Marriage
content: A woman who negotiated a divorce settlement and then changed her mind about the divorce, had her marriage dissolved by an irked Supreme Court, which said changing one's mind at that stage is unacceptable.

title: Wetzel: For the good of WNBA, commish needs thicker skin
content: the WNBA has two new expansion franchises (Portland and Toronto) it has a new labor deal and its biggest and most widespread media rights deal ever . it has superstar A'ja Wilson seeking to lead her Las Vegas Aces to a fourth title in five years . veteran-led rosters in new york, Minnesota and elsewhere are eager to stop them .

title: Inside the end of the Ja Morant era in Memphis
content: JA MORANT closed within an arm's length and launched off two feet in the baby blue paint . the 6-foot-8 Dyson Daniels took off for a two-handed dunk . despite a 5-inch height disadvantage, Morant managed to swipe the ball from behind .

title: Are the Dodgers this good? Are the Mets this bad? What we learned from MLB's $1 billion series
content: the Los Angeles Dodgers and New York Mets have a combined payroll of more than $1 billion . but only one of them is getting their money's worth so far . the Mets fell to 7-12 and are in last place in the national league East .
